title: Implement compensatable cart merge lifecycle
verify: No-target mode transfers the source to the actor and keeps it active.
Existing-target mode applies absolute quantities under sorted locks, then soft-deletes the source before journal completion.
Injected failure after source soft-delete restores the source before reversing target mutations.
Stock or mutation failure leaves both carts usable and the journal non-completed.
T3 closure includes /verify PASS, per-task semantic-pass, human checkpoint, and rollback/recovery evidence.

verify.summary: The required packet and deterministic lifecycle gates passed, but functional verification failed against the linked SDD contract. Target line updates/additions and ownership transfer call Cart Module mutations directly, and new target lines copy source price snapshots. The authoritative cart-runtime spec and TASK-020 constraints require Medusa core cart workflows for these mutations. Current evidence does not prove target/customer pricing, tax, promotion, and refresh behavior remains correct.

The core-workflow remediation satisfies the linked SDD mutation boundary: ownership transfer uses Medusa core workflow, target line mutation uses add-to-cart plus forced refresh, direct Cart Module mutation is limited to source soft-delete/restore, and integration evidence covers exact quantities, pricing snapshot isolation, totals, taxes, promotions, journal ordering, compensation, retry, and stock-conflict behavior. T3 closure remains pending per-task /red-verify semantic-pass and HUMAN_CHECKPOINT: done.

Adversarial semantic verification found a cross-task contract mismatch. TASK-019 planning and linked SDD specs aggregate source and target lines by variant_id, but TASK-020 runtime rejects target carts containing more than one line for the planned target variant. A valid planned same-variant target state can therefore fail before merge instead of committing exact summed quantities or compensating, so TASK-020 is not T3 closure-eligible.

Functional verification passed after duplicate target variant-line remediation. TASK-020 now validates target quantities by aggregate Product Variant ID, preserves planned anchor-line identity, keeps target mutation in Medusa add-to-cart plus forced refresh workflows, and lifecycle evidence includes targetDuplicateVariantLinesMerged: true. T3 closure remains pending repeated per-task /red-verify semantic-pass and HUMAN_CHECKPOINT: done.

Adversarial semantic verification passed after duplicate target variant-line remediation. TASK-019 planning, linked SDD aggregation semantics, and TASK-020 lifecycle now align: duplicate target same-variant lines are accepted as aggregate target quantity, compensated, and merged to the exact final quantity through Medusa core workflows. At red-verification time TASK-020 remained not done because HUMAN_CHECKPOINT: done was still pending.

verify.closure_decision: Manual T3 closure explicitly requested by the user after /verify PASS and per-task /red-verify semantic-pass.
verify.summary: GENERAL closed TASK-020 in manual mode after confirming the required T3 gates: full protocol evidence, required packet/spec gates, repeated functional /verify PASS, repeated per-task /red-verify semantic-pass, ROLLBACK_RECOVERY_NOTE: present, and explicit HUMAN_CHECKPOINT: done. No dependent task was promoted during mb-sync.

evidence_required: Transfer and existing-target lifecycle integration output.
Source soft-delete, target quantity, and journal-ordering evidence.
T3 human checkpoint and rollback/recovery note before closure.
constraints: Use supported Medusa core workflows plus Cart Module softDeleteCarts/restoreCarts.
Never hard-delete or clear the source cart.
Return no success before target, source disposition, and journal state are durable.
invariants: Failure cannot lose or duplicate source or target quantities.
Journal completed implies existing-target source is soft-deleted.
Compensation restores source before target reversal.
purpose: Implement the no-data-loss mutation lifecycle independently from HTTP authentication concerns.
success_outcome: A planned merge either commits exact target quantities with the source consumed or restores both carts to their pre-merge state.
anti_goals: Do not expose the Store merge route.
Do not hard-delete or empty the source.
Do not implement OAuth, checkout, order, reservation, or payment.

forbidden_scope: hard deletion or clearing of source cart data
stop_conditions: Supported Cart Module soft-delete/restore cannot provide reversible source disposition.
A failure path can leave untracked partial target mutations.
Journal completion cannot be ordered after source soft-delete.
